After all, the core idea of Android is that it's open source. To make matters worse, there are politics at play. The best smartphone hardware over the past few years has undeniably come from Chinese phone brands, but there are chunks of people who would never use Chinese phones, partially because of not wanting to be associated with the Chinese government and also because they're tougher to get outside the country.

Android needs to cater to enthusiasts

Honestly, I don't see Android phone brands ever matching Apple in brand cachet or ecosystem. The only option for Android flagships to survive is to pivot even harder toward power users and enthusiasts with innovative, niche products. This means focusing on foldable, camera-centric phones at a greater scale. The likes of Samsung and Xiaomi should consider releasing lower-priced versions of foldable phones. Give it a plastic/rubber frame and older SoC if you need to, but a Galaxy Fold FE at $799 could easily entice users to jump on board. Android brands should also try more form factors, maybe a real gaming phone with buttons and joystick built into the body; or a dual-screen phone; or one with a swivel screen (yes, I am aware Microsoft and LG released those exact devices and didn't sell, but Microsoft and LG also have/had terrible Android software with poor marketing. Maybe if Google or Samsung try them they'd fare better).

Whatever the case, I think Samsung and Xiaomi trying to release base-level flagships like the Galaxy S23 and Xiaomi 13 and trying to compete directly against Apple is a lost cause. The Android phones still garnering buzz and some sales are the top-tier Ultras with Leica-branded cameras or phones that fold with stylus support.

Of course, none of this may work. I have no experience running a company or selling products. But whatever the case, the iPhone is slowly winning the whole mobile war, and Android brands have got to try something different before it's too late.
item.content:encoded:
During a press conference addressing the recent surge of car thefts in New York City in May, Mayor Eric Adams urged citizens to buy AirTags for their cars. When told by a reporter that AirTags do not work with Android phones, Adams responded, "Why would anyone have [an Android phone]?" His ignorant response — there are over 130 million Android users in the U.S., according to the most recent research — was only met with a bit of backlash, mostly on Twitter and a couple Android sites. The mayor of one of the biggest cities in America giving ridiculous advice and dismissing valid concerns wasn't that big of a deal because many Americans felt the same way.

The iPhone is so dominant in America that it's considered the default best phone. In American culture, there are memes mocking Android users, news reports of teens feeling left out at school if they're on an Android, and people being left off family group chats if they're not on iMessage. Android phones are considered second-grade devices to many Americans.

But as mentioned earlier, there are, in fact, lots of Android users in America. But at the risk of overgeneralizing, we can deduce by looking at flagship phone sales and anecdotes that American Android users tend to fall into two groups: mobile enthusiasts (phone nerds, like people who frequent XDA forums) or those on a tighter budget. But Android could use that to its advantage if it wants to succeed at a similar level as Apple has.

Just how popular is the iPhone?

Most average Americans with means or disposable income default to the iPhone, including a whopping 87% of American teens from 2021 research. The iPhone enjoys a similar dominant status throughout Western Europe, to no one's surprise. It's easy to assume the iPhone wouldn't be as dominant in Asia because it's home to three countries — China, South Korea, and Japan — with major conglomerates that make their own smartphones.

But actually, the iPhone has an even higher market share in Japan than in America, and the iPhone is gaining stream in China and South Korea as well, a surge led by millennials and Gen Z. While research figures still have Huawei and Samsung sitting at the top of the market share in China and South Korean, respectively, the reality is those figures are propped up by mid-range or budget devices used by the elderly or lower-income populations. Among young, well-to-do Chinese and Koreans in major cities, the iPhone has become the phone of choice. I went to Beijing, Shanghai, and Seoul last month, and noticed mostly iPhones in the hands of people who populate trendy areas. Virtually every influencer I've seen in Asia carry an iPhone. In fact, the Korean Herald wrote a story recently on the same trend. More young Koreans are choosing the iPhone over the Galaxy. In fact, young people choosing iPhone appears to be the case worldwide, according to a recent Wall Street Journal report.

Android phones are considered second-grade devices to many Americans.

Recent sales figures provide further evidence. Recent Counterpoint research shows phone sales have fallen year-over-year for the third straight year, with only Apple seeing meaningful growth. Another report by Canalys reported that, of the top 15 flagship phones sold worldwide in Q1 of 2023, the iPhones took seven spots, including the first four.

Source: Canalys

This all paints a really grim picture for Android flagships, which are bending over backwards fighting each other for scraps of the global market share. If Samsung, Huawei, and Oppo can't even win their home countries' market share, what hope is there globally? This makes me, a mobile enthusiast who prefers Android phones because they push hardware innovation at a faster pace, concerned for the future. Ultimately, these companies are running a business, and if no one is buying flagship Android phones, then they'll eventually stop making them, which will bring new designs and innovation to a halt.

What can Android brands do to make a comeback?

Android brands should focus on foldables and power user phones.

If your answer to how Android bands can make a comeback is for them to "make better phones," then you haven't been paying attention to the phone scene. Android flagships have the best cameras and displays, the former by a significant amount. Android brands also offer comparatively better value than iPhones; Chinese brands often include screen protectors and cases and chargers with their flagships, while everything is a separate purchase with iPhones. But people still choose to buy iPhones anyway due to a myriad of factors that go beyond it being a "better phone."

Apple is a status symbol among some people. In Asian cultures, where there's a more superficial emphasis on brand names, the Apple logo alone is a selling point. My former XDA colleague Sumukh Rao tweeted the following.

This isn't the first time I've heard an Indian colleague complain that people in India see using iPhones as a social status signifier. And this mentality is also true in China and the U.S. There's a viral meme on TikTok right now in which young women are asked, "he's a 10, but he uses an Android," and the women would recoil and then answer, "he drops to a 4." My friend, a Pixel user, says he's experienced this in the online dating scene, too. He said when they moved the chat from the app over to texting, a couple of women pointed out that he used Android.

Of course, not all iPhone users are on it for superficial reasons. Some genuinely like Apple's products and services, and the company does a very good job of locking users in once they dive into the walled garden. I think the Apple Watch is the best smartwatch by a significant margin, but it only works with an iPhone. The AirPods Pro are also one of the top two or three best earbuds I've tested, and they too, play much nicer with iPhones. The synergy between Apple hardware and software is unmatched in the consumer tech space right now, and it helps to convince the average consumer to just stick with all Apple products.

The Apple Watch is great, and it’s locked to the iPhone

I'm not sure if there's anything Android brands can do to combat Apple's reputation as a status symbol. Steve Jobs is too iconic, and the Apple branding is too well entrenched. Android brands can try to match Apple's ecosystem, and some brands such as Samsung and Huawei have built good ones, but it's hard for these brands to lock their consumers in the way Apple can, partly because they don't move enough units and also because Android users are much more likely to push back against restrictions. After all, the core idea of Android is that it's open source. To make matters worse, there are politics at play. The best smartphone hardware over the past few years has undeniably come from Chinese phone brands, but there are chunks of people who would never use Chinese phones, partially because of not wanting to be associated with the Chinese government and also because they're tougher to get outside the country.

Android needs to cater to enthusiasts

Honestly, I don't see Android phone brands ever matching Apple in brand cachet or ecosystem. The only option for Android flagships to survive is to pivot even harder toward power users and enthusiasts with innovative, niche products. This means focusing on foldable, camera-centric phones at a greater scale. The likes of Samsung and Xiaomi should consider releasing lower-priced versions of foldable phones. Give it a plastic/rubber frame and older SoC if you need to, but a Galaxy Fold FE at $799 could easily entice users to jump on board. Android brands should also try more form factors, maybe a real gaming phone with buttons and joystick built into the body; or a dual-screen phone; or one with a swivel screen (yes, I am aware Microsoft and LG released those exact devices and didn't sell, but Microsoft and LG also have/had terrible Android software with poor marketing. Maybe if Google or Samsung try them they'd fare better).

Whatever the case, I think Samsung and Xiaomi trying to release base-level flagships like the Galaxy S23 and Xiaomi 13 and trying to compete directly against Apple is a lost cause. The Android phones still garnering buzz and some sales are the top-tier Ultras with Leica-branded cameras or phones that fold with stylus support.

Of course, none of this may work. I have no experience running a company or selling products. But whatever the case, the iPhone is slowly winning the whole mobile war, and Android brands have got to try something different before it's too late.

item.title: Best OLED laptops in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best OLED laptops in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
When you think about the best phones you can buy today, the most expensive, flagship devices often have OLED displays. This type of panel allows displays to be more vibrant and more colorful, but they can also deliver true blacks and potentially save power. It's taken some time for OLED displays to become more common on laptops, but today, many of the best laptops offer that option. OLED is still mostly a premium feature, so most of the laptops available come with a fairly high price tag, but that also means they're great in just about every way. Here are some of our favorites.

  • Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
    Best overall
    $1150 at Lenovo
  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best lightweight laptop
    $1449 at Amazon
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
    Great alternative
    $1450 at Dell
  • Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED (2023)
    Great for gamers
    $2199 at Amazon
  • Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
    Best business laptop
    $1275 at Lenovo
  • Dell XPS 15 (2023)
    Best 15-inch laptop
    $2000 at Dell
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED (2023)
    Best for creators
    $2200 at B&H
  • Asus Vivobook 15 OLED (2023)
    Best budget laptop
    $600 at Newegg

The best laptops with OLED displays in 2023

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best overall

The best laptop on the market

$1150 $1400 Save $250

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is the best consumer laptop on the market, featuring 13th-generation Intel processors and a stunning OLED display in two different configurations. It also has a solid 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support and a beautiful shiny design.

Pros
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel Core i7 processor
  • Stunning OLED display with either 2.8K or 4K options
  • Gorgeous metal design with shiny edges
Cons
  • No place to attach a pen if needed
$1150 at Lenovo $1400 at Best Buy

The 2022 Lenovo Yoga 9i was our favorite laptop of that year, and the 2023 model is repeating that feat by simply improving on the essentials.

Lenovo only really changed the performance for this year's model, upgrading to an Intel Core i7-1360P, with 12 cores and 16 threads that deliver fantastic performance for day-to-day tasks. That processor also includes Intel Iris Xe graphics, though that's basically the same as last year. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, all you need for a great experience.

However, you came here to talk about the display. Lenovo is still using a 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, but it has done away with the IPS panel option this year. Now, the Yoga 9i comes with a 2.8K OLED 90Hz panel by default, though you can upgrade to a 4K 60Hz display if you prefer that. The screen supports touch and pen input, as expected for a convertible. The Lenovo Yoga 9i also has a fantastic audio system with a soundbar built into the hinge, so the media experience is fantastic overall. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, too.

As for the design, it's exactly the same as last year, but that's not a bad thing. It's an aluminum laptop with matte surfaces and shiny polished edges that make it look gorgeous, both in Storm Grey and Oatmeal color options. It's fairly light, too, coming in at 3.09 pounds. Lenovo also packed a decent number of ports in here, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, and a headphone jack, which is solid for a premium laptop these days.

At the end of the day, the 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i gets a ton right. It has a stunning design, an even better display, plenty of performance, and more. It's one of the best laptops on the market, and with two great OLED display options, it more than earns its place on this list.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best lightweight laptop

A stunning OLED display in a portable chassis

$1450 $1750 Save $300

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro has one of the best AMOLED displays on any laptop, with sharp 2.8K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has high-end Intel processors and other specs, all wrapped in a thin premium chassis.

Pros
  • 2.8K AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate
  • Premium aluminum build and a surprisingly thin chassis
  • More ports than most competing premium laptops
Cons
  • No Windows Hello facial recognition
  • HDMI output doesn't support 4K at 60Hz
$1450 at Samsung $1449 at Amazon $1450 at Best Buy (14 inches) $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches)

Samsung has been making great OLED laptops for a couple of years now, but the Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a major step forward in multiple ways, and it earns its place as one of the very best of its kind.

Inside the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro are 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i7-1360P with 12 cores and 16 threads, delivering solid performance for all kinds of tasks. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD for storage, not too far off from the Yoga 9i above.

The biggest upgrade the Galaxy Book 3 Pro delivers compared to its predecessor is the Dynamic AMOLED 2X display. There are 14-inch and 16-inch versions of this laptop, but both now come with a 16:10 aspect ratio and a super sharp 2.8K resolution, a massive improvement in quality and usability. On top of that, the 120Hz refresh rate makes everything appear even smoother. The webcam is the same, though: a 1080p sensor without Windows Hello support (though there's a fingerprint reader for that).

Another notable change with this model is the design, with Samsung now using an aluminum chassis instead of magnesium. This results in a much more premium feel, but it makes the laptop a bit heavier than its predecessor, at 2.58 pounds for the 14-inch and 3.44 pounds for the 16-inch model. Both sizes come in Graphite or Beige colorways, and the larger model has a number pad. There's a great supply of ports on both, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. The HDMI port doesn't support 4K 60Hz displays, but it's nice to have HDMI at all, considering many premium laptops skip it.

Overall, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a fantastic laptop, and while the 16-inch version is a bit on the larger side, you can go with either model as both are extremely portable.

Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
Great alternative

An ultra-modern clamshell laptop with a stunning display

The 2023 Dell XPS 13 Plus comes with upgraded 13th-generation Intel Core processors while keeping the futuristic and sleek design it's known for, including an invisible touchpad and a stunning 3.5K OLED display as a configuration option.

Pros
  • Super-sharp 3.5K OLED display option
  • Great performance with 13th-gen Intel Core processors
  • Futuristic design looks incredibly sleek
Cons
  • The invisible touchpad and function row may take some getting used to
  • Limited ports
  • 720p webcam
$1450 at Dell $1950 at Best Buy

Dell's XPS lineup is one of the most popular among Windows laptops, and after the completely redesigned XPS 13 Plus in 2022, the 2023 model is a small refresh, but it's still a great choice.

The only thing that's really changed this year is the processor, which now comes with up to an Intel Core i7-1360P, a 12-core, 16-thread CPU that should deliver a nice performance bump over the 2022 model, thanks to boost speeds going up to 5GHz. Otherwise, you can go up to 32GB of RAM (which is faster now) and a 2TB SSD for storage.

Unlike the laptops above, the Dell XPS 13 Plus doesn't come with an OLED display by default, but it does give you the option for a fantastic 3.5K OLED. For a 13.4-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio, that's incredibly sharp, and the screen supports touch, too. Unfortunately, the webcam, is still a 720p sensor, something Dell refuses to fix with its XPS laptops. It does support Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

After a major design shift for the 2022 model, this year's Dell XPS 13 Plus stays the same, but it still feels super modern. Features like the invisible touchpad, zero-lattice keyboard, and touch function row make this an extremely sleek laptop that looks great in both Platinum and Graphite colorways. It's a light laptop, too, weighing 2.77 pounds for the OLED model. Ports are very limited, though, with just two Thunderbolt 4 ports out of the box. You get adapters in the box for USB Type-A and a headphone jack if you need them, though this still isn't ideal.

While the futuristic design comes with some compromises, the Dell XPS 13 Plus is a phenomenal laptop in most ways, and that stunning 3.5K OLED displays pushes it even higher. We've reviewed the 2022 model, which is identical in many ways.

Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED (2023)
Great for gamers

Make your games look and run their best

$2199 $2299 Save $100

The Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED has a powerful Intel Core processor and the latest GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards paired with a 4K OLED display for an all-around great gaming experience. Unlike most gaming laptops, it's pretty thin and has a fairly minimalist design.

Pros
  • Fast Intel Core processors
  • Plenty of ports
  • Fairly thin despite its powerful specs
Cons
  • Refresh rate can be higher
  • Expensive
$2199 at Amazon $2199 at Newegg

With their vibrant colors, high contrast ratios, and wide viewing angles, OLED displays serve as great additions to gaming laptops. Unfortunately, there has been a noticeable lack of proper gaming-oriented laptops that feature OLED panels this year. Although the Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED is built like a workstation, this powerhouse of an OLED laptop is capable of running the most graphically intensive games with ease.

Performance-wise, you get what you pay for, with the base model housing a powerful Intel Core i7-13700H processor. The 14-core and 20-thread CPU boasts a maximum clock speed of 5.00GHz to deliver solid performance for all your gaming needs. But if you want even more horsepower out of your system, you can go for the Intel Core i9-13900H, which has a slightly higher clock speed of 5.40 GHz. The cheapest Aero 16 laptop comes with a modest RTX 4060 graphics card, but you can upgrade to an RTX 4070 GPU if you don't wish to turn down the graphical settings in your favorite games. The laptop also comes with 32GB DDR5 RAM and 1TB of Gen4 M.2 SSD storage to provide a well-rounded gaming experience.

The display on the Gigabyte Aero 16 is an OLED panel with a screen resolution 3840x2400, so it's pretty sharp. The tall 16:10 aspect ratio is also a plus-point, though the 60Hz refresh rate leaves a lot to be desired. Luckily, the laptop doesn't skimp out on the webcam as you get a 1080p front camera that supports Windows Hello face authentication.

The laptop weighs 4.19 pounds, and while its thickness can vary depending on its configuration, a maximum of 22mm isn't bad for a laptop of its caliber. The lack of gamer-esque aesthetics is a welcome addition, and its beautiful silver chassis has a decent number of ports. You get three USB Type-C connections, a USB Type-A port, a microSD card reader, an HDMI connection and even an audio jack, though users of LAN connections might want to invest in a dock as the laptop lacks a dedicated RJ45 Ethernet port.

The Gigabyte Aero 16 may not be built like your typical flashy gaming laptop, but it's easily our top pick for a high-performance OLED battlestation.

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

A beautiful screen for getting work done

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon may be the most well-known business laptop on the market, and for good reason. It has fast Intel processors and packs a solid 2.8K OLED display, all while retaining the sleek and premium business-style design ThinkPads are known for.

Pros
  • Optional 2.8K OLED display
  • 13th-generation Intel Core U- or P-series processors
  • Classic ThinkPad design is great for business
Cons
  • ThinkPad design may not be suitable for more modern audiences
  • RAM is soldered onto the laptop
$1275 at Lenovo $1720 at Best Buy $1659 at Newegg

Lenovo's ThinkPad X1 Carbon is one of the best and most iconic business laptops ever, and while the Gen 11 model doesn't change much, it's still one of the very best you can buy.

Starting with performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel Core processors, this time with the option to choose up to a Core i7-1365U or a Core i7-1370P, both supporting vPro Enterprise technologies. The U-series processors are more power-efficient than the P-series models, but they're not as fast, so you can choose based on whether you want more performance or better battery life. The laptop can also be configured with up to 64GB of RAM (which is soldered) and a 2TB SSD.

Most of the display options have remained the same, and while most configurations of the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 come with an IPS panel, you still have the option to choose a 2.8K OLED display. This is extremely sharp for a 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and of course, it has the usual benefits of OLED. You also get a 1080p webcam right above that display, with the option to add Windows Hello and Computer Vision support.

As you'd expect, the design is basically unchanged, with the black surfaces and red accents you know and (possibly) love from previous ThinkPads. It's a look that's probably divisive, but it's loved by a lot of people. What's a clear win is the portability, seeing as the ThinkPad X1 Carbon weighs just 2.48 pounds. There's a good setup of ports, too, including two Thunderbolt 4 and two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack. And if you need internet wherever you go, you can also add LTE or 5G connectivity.

It's no surprise by now, but the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is one of the best business laptops on the market, and the OLED display makes it even sweeter. We've reviewed the Gen 10 model, which is very similar aside from having older processors.

Dell XPS 15 (2023)
Best 15-inch laptop

Powerful performance in a svelte chassis

The Dell XPS 15 is one of the best 15-inch laptops, offering a great combination of performance and portability. It has powerful Intel H-series processors and up to Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 graphics packed into a relatively thin design, and of course, an optional 3.5K OLED display.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core H-series processors and Nvidia RTX graphics
  • Optional 3.5K OLED display looks stunning
  • Slim chassis
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • A little heavy
  • Design is starting to feel a little dated
$2000 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

The Dell XPS 15 has consistently been one of the best 15-inch laptops on the market, and that status remains true with the 2023 model, even though not much has changed.

Performance is the area where Dell put most of its focus this time around, upgrading to 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i9-13900H with 14 cores and 20 threads. But the real upgrade is actually the GPU, with the transition to Nvidia's RTX 40 series, up to an RTX 4070 with 8GB of memory (the 2022 model maxed out with an RTX 3050 Ti). This will make a big difference for content creation and even gaming, plus the laptop also comes with up to 64GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

Nothing else has changed with the 2023 model, but that's not necessarily bad. The 15.6-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio gives you the option for a stunning 3.5K OLED panel, which covers 100% of DCI-P3. Plus, a quad-speaker setup with up to 10W of peak output delivers fantastic audio, too. Sadly, the webcam is bad, being the same 720p sensor Dell has been using for years. It does have Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

For how powerful the Dell XPS 15 is, it's not overly large, measuring 18mm in thickness and weighing 4.23 pounds. This year, there's only one color option, with a silver exterior and black carbon fiber interior. As for ports, the setup is the same as before, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, a standard USB-C port, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader. You also get an adapter for USB Type-A and HDMI if you need those ports.

Aside from the stagnant design language, the 2023 Dell XPS 15 is still one of the very best laptops on the market, and it's more powerful than ever.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED (2023)
Best for creators

Enhance your productivity with an OLED screen

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED is a powerful laptop for creators, with 13th-generation Intel Core HX-series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics. It also has a built-in Asus dial, providing quick granular controls in certain media apps.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel Core processors and Nvidia 40-series GPUs
  • 1080p front camera with Windows Hello
  • 16:10 aspect ratio and 120Hz refresh rate
Cons
  • Default 16GB RAM can be a bottleneck
$2500 at Amazon $2500 at Best Buy $2200 at B&H

Aside from the processor, an OLED screen is perhaps the most valued feature in the photo and video editing community. There are plenty of decent OLED laptops on the market, but not a single one comes close to the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16.

Starting with the specs, the ProArt StudioBook 16 is armed with Intel's Core i9-13980HX CPU that delivers strong performance with its 5.6GHz turbo clock speed and a whooping 24 cores and 32 threads. This top-of-the-line processor is paired with an Nvidia RTX 4060 graphics card, and you can go for the high-end model with an RTX 4070 for a solid boost to your video editing and graphics rendering workloads. The default 16GB DDR5 memory can a bit on the lower side for hardware-intensive workloads, but Asus allows you to upgrade the RAM to 64GB. You also get a 1TB NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD to store all your productivity apps.

Display-wise, you get a 16-inch OLED panel with a 3200x2000 resolution and a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. Besides its 120Hz refresh rate, the touchscreen display on the ProArt StudioBook 16 boasts 100% DCI-P3 color gamut, and it supports MPP 2.0 styluses with up to 4096 pressure levels. On top of that, the laptop includes a 1080p front camera with Windows Hello for added security.

At 5.29 pounds, it's not the most portable workstation on the market, but that is to be expected considering its high-end specs. The 2023 model of the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 has limited color options, but its mineral black body offers plenty of ports, including two USB Type-A connections, two USB Type-C ports, an audio jack, a RJ45 Ethernet port, an HDMI connection, and even an SD card reader. The built-in Asus Dial increases your productivity by giving you more intuitive controls for certain apps.

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 may be a bit on the pricey side, but it's the best OLED laptop if you wish to get into content creation.

Asus Vivobook 15 OLED (2023)
Best budget laptop

A beautiful viewing experience on the cheap

$600 $700 Save $100

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED may be an affordable laptop, but it still packs AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors and an OLED display that makes it great for consuming media like movies and TV. It also looks fairly sleek, and it's decently light for its size.

Pros
  • AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors
  • OLED display in the base configuration
  • Decently light considering its size
Cons
  • No USB4 support
  • 720p webcam
  • Doesn't support DDR5 memory
$850 at Best Buy $677 at Amazon $600 at Newegg

OLED displays sued to be reserved for premium laptops, but the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED brings that great viewing experience to a much lower price point, all while being a decently modern laptop.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is powered by AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors, specifically starting with a Ryzen 7 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads. That already gives you solid day-to-day performance, and the laptop also includes 8GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD to round things out. It gives you a pretty good experience for the price.

But of course, the main draw here is that display, and even with a low price tag of $700, the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED comes with a nice 15.6-inch OLED display. It does come in a more traditional aspect ratio and Full HD resolution, but this is still a fantastic screen for watching movies and shows on, especially since it covers 100% of DCI-P3 and supports HDR. The webcam is a bit of a downside, being a 720p sensor without facial recognition, though at least the laptop has a fingerprint sensor for Windows Hello.

Design isn't really a priority with an affordable laptop, but the Vivobook 15 OLED still manages to look sleek and the 3.75 pounds of weight are not outlandish for a 15-inch laptop. It even passes MIL-STD-810H durability testing, which is really impressive. As for ports, it has one USB Type-C and three Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so there's a good bit of variety here.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is a surprisingly good laptop for the price, and getting such a good OLED display for $700 makes this a bargain. It may not have incredible performance, but for day-to-day use and watching movies, it's fantastic.

Best OLED laptops in 2023: Bottom line

It's certainly great to see OLED displays become more of a mainstay in the laptop market. While this used to be a very premium feature, these types of panels are more widely available than ever, and you can get them for very cheap. The best experience comes from the Lenovo Yoga 9i, which not only has a stunning display, but also a beautifully-crafted chassis, top-tier performance, a fantastic audio experience, and a solid webcam, all packed into a package that's still decently portable. Plus, it's a convertible, and more versatility is always a good thing.

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best overall

The best laptop on the market

$1150 $1400 Save $250

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is the best consumer laptop on the market, featuring 13th-generation Intel processors and a stunning OLED display in two different configurations. It also has a solid 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support and a beautiful shiny design.

Pros
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel Core i7 processor
  • Stunning OLED display with either 2.8K or 4K options
  • Gorgeous metal design with shiny edges
Cons
  • No place to attach a pen if needed
$1150 at Lenovo $1400 at Best Buy

With that being said, it's a fairly expensive machine, and thankfully, OLED displays come trickled down to very affordable laptops like the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED. It may not have all the bells and whistles, but it does have a beautiful OLED display with HDR support and great colors for an incredibly low price, and it doesn't make a ton of sacrifices to get there. It still has solid performance and a reasonably light design, so it's really a fantastic choice for the price.

But if you want a thin and light companion with an OLED display, then you should look no further than the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro. Despite being portable, it's a highly capable laptop with a solid Intel Core i7-1360P processor and a stunning 2.8K AMOLED display.
item.description:
When you think about the best phones you can buy today, the most expensive, flagship devices often have OLED displays. This type of panel allows displays to be more vibrant and more colorful, but they can also deliver true blacks and potentially save power. It's taken some time for OLED displays to become more common on laptops, but today, many of the best laptops offer that option. OLED is still mostly a premium feature, so most of the laptops available come with a fairly high price tag, but that also means they're great in just about every way. Here are some of our favorites.

  • Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
    Best overall
    $1150 at Lenovo
  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best lightweight laptop
    $1449 at Amazon
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
    Great alternative
    $1450 at Dell
  • Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED (2023)
    Great for gamers
    $2199 at Amazon
  • Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
    Best business laptop
    $1275 at Lenovo
  • Dell XPS 15 (2023)
    Best 15-inch laptop
    $2000 at Dell
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED (2023)
    Best for creators
    $2200 at B&H
  • Asus Vivobook 15 OLED (2023)
    Best budget laptop
    $600 at Newegg

The best laptops with OLED displays in 2023

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best overall

The best laptop on the market

$1150 $1400 Save $250

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is the best consumer laptop on the market, featuring 13th-generation Intel processors and a stunning OLED display in two different configurations. It also has a solid 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support and a beautiful shiny design.

Pros
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel Core i7 processor
  • Stunning OLED display with either 2.8K or 4K options
  • Gorgeous metal design with shiny edges
Cons
  • No place to attach a pen if needed
$1150 at Lenovo $1400 at Best Buy

The 2022 Lenovo Yoga 9i was our favorite laptop of that year, and the 2023 model is repeating that feat by simply improving on the essentials.

Lenovo only really changed the performance for this year's model, upgrading to an Intel Core i7-1360P, with 12 cores and 16 threads that deliver fantastic performance for day-to-day tasks. That processor also includes Intel Iris Xe graphics, though that's basically the same as last year. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, all you need for a great experience.

However, you came here to talk about the display. Lenovo is still using a 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, but it has done away with the IPS panel option this year. Now, the Yoga 9i comes with a 2.8K OLED 90Hz panel by default, though you can upgrade to a 4K 60Hz display if you prefer that. The screen supports touch and pen input, as expected for a convertible. The Lenovo Yoga 9i also has a fantastic audio system with a soundbar built into the hinge, so the media experience is fantastic overall. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, too.

As for the design, it's exactly the same as last year, but that's not a bad thing. It's an aluminum laptop with matte surfaces and shiny polished edges that make it look gorgeous, both in Storm Grey and Oatmeal color options. It's fairly light, too, coming in at 3.09 pounds. Lenovo also packed a decent number of ports in here, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, and a headphone jack, which is solid for a premium laptop these days.

At the end of the day, the 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i gets a ton right. It has a stunning design, an even better display, plenty of performance, and more. It's one of the best laptops on the market, and with two great OLED display options, it more than earns its place on this list.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best lightweight laptop

A stunning OLED display in a portable chassis

$1450 $1750 Save $300

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro has one of the best AMOLED displays on any laptop, with sharp 2.8K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has high-end Intel processors and other specs, all wrapped in a thin premium chassis.

Pros
  • 2.8K AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate
  • Premium aluminum build and a surprisingly thin chassis
  • More ports than most competing premium laptops
Cons
  • No Windows Hello facial recognition
  • HDMI output doesn't support 4K at 60Hz
$1450 at Samsung $1449 at Amazon $1450 at Best Buy (14 inches) $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches)

Samsung has been making great OLED laptops for a couple of years now, but the Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a major step forward in multiple ways, and it earns its place as one of the very best of its kind.

Inside the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro are 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i7-1360P with 12 cores and 16 threads, delivering solid performance for all kinds of tasks. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD for storage, not too far off from the Yoga 9i above.

The biggest upgrade the Galaxy Book 3 Pro delivers compared to its predecessor is the Dynamic AMOLED 2X display. There are 14-inch and 16-inch versions of this laptop, but both now come with a 16:10 aspect ratio and a super sharp 2.8K resolution, a massive improvement in quality and usability. On top of that, the 120Hz refresh rate makes everything appear even smoother. The webcam is the same, though: a 1080p sensor without Windows Hello support (though there's a fingerprint reader for that).

Another notable change with this model is the design, with Samsung now using an aluminum chassis instead of magnesium. This results in a much more premium feel, but it makes the laptop a bit heavier than its predecessor, at 2.58 pounds for the 14-inch and 3.44 pounds for the 16-inch model. Both sizes come in Graphite or Beige colorways, and the larger model has a number pad. There's a great supply of ports on both, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. The HDMI port doesn't support 4K 60Hz displays, but it's nice to have HDMI at all, considering many premium laptops skip it.

Overall, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a fantastic laptop, and while the 16-inch version is a bit on the larger side, you can go with either model as both are extremely portable.

Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
Great alternative

An ultra-modern clamshell laptop with a stunning display

The 2023 Dell XPS 13 Plus comes with upgraded 13th-generation Intel Core processors while keeping the futuristic and sleek design it's known for, including an invisible touchpad and a stunning 3.5K OLED display as a configuration option.

Pros
  • Super-sharp 3.5K OLED display option
  • Great performance with 13th-gen Intel Core processors
  • Futuristic design looks incredibly sleek
Cons
  • The invisible touchpad and function row may take some getting used to
  • Limited ports
  • 720p webcam
$1450 at Dell $1950 at Best Buy

Dell's XPS lineup is one of the most popular among Windows laptops, and after the completely redesigned XPS 13 Plus in 2022, the 2023 model is a small refresh, but it's still a great choice.

The only thing that's really changed this year is the processor, which now comes with up to an Intel Core i7-1360P, a 12-core, 16-thread CPU that should deliver a nice performance bump over the 2022 model, thanks to boost speeds going up to 5GHz. Otherwise, you can go up to 32GB of RAM (which is faster now) and a 2TB SSD for storage.

Unlike the laptops above, the Dell XPS 13 Plus doesn't come with an OLED display by default, but it does give you the option for a fantastic 3.5K OLED. For a 13.4-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio, that's incredibly sharp, and the screen supports touch, too. Unfortunately, the webcam, is still a 720p sensor, something Dell refuses to fix with its XPS laptops. It does support Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

After a major design shift for the 2022 model, this year's Dell XPS 13 Plus stays the same, but it still feels super modern. Features like the invisible touchpad, zero-lattice keyboard, and touch function row make this an extremely sleek laptop that looks great in both Platinum and Graphite colorways. It's a light laptop, too, weighing 2.77 pounds for the OLED model. Ports are very limited, though, with just two Thunderbolt 4 ports out of the box. You get adapters in the box for USB Type-A and a headphone jack if you need them, though this still isn't ideal.

While the futuristic design comes with some compromises, the Dell XPS 13 Plus is a phenomenal laptop in most ways, and that stunning 3.5K OLED displays pushes it even higher. We've reviewed the 2022 model, which is identical in many ways.

Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED (2023)
Great for gamers

Make your games look and run their best

$2199 $2299 Save $100

The Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED has a powerful Intel Core processor and the latest GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards paired with a 4K OLED display for an all-around great gaming experience. Unlike most gaming laptops, it's pretty thin and has a fairly minimalist design.

Pros
  • Fast Intel Core processors
  • Plenty of ports
  • Fairly thin despite its powerful specs
Cons
  • Refresh rate can be higher
  • Expensive
$2199 at Amazon $2199 at Newegg

With their vibrant colors, high contrast ratios, and wide viewing angles, OLED displays serve as great additions to gaming laptops. Unfortunately, there has been a noticeable lack of proper gaming-oriented laptops that feature OLED panels this year. Although the Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED is built like a workstation, this powerhouse of an OLED laptop is capable of running the most graphically intensive games with ease.

Performance-wise, you get what you pay for, with the base model housing a powerful Intel Core i7-13700H processor. The 14-core and 20-thread CPU boasts a maximum clock speed of 5.00GHz to deliver solid performance for all your gaming needs. But if you want even more horsepower out of your system, you can go for the Intel Core i9-13900H, which has a slightly higher clock speed of 5.40 GHz. The cheapest Aero 16 laptop comes with a modest RTX 4060 graphics card, but you can upgrade to an RTX 4070 GPU if you don't wish to turn down the graphical settings in your favorite games. The laptop also comes with 32GB DDR5 RAM and 1TB of Gen4 M.2 SSD storage to provide a well-rounded gaming experience.

The display on the Gigabyte Aero 16 is an OLED panel with a screen resolution 3840x2400, so it's pretty sharp. The tall 16:10 aspect ratio is also a plus-point, though the 60Hz refresh rate leaves a lot to be desired. Luckily, the laptop doesn't skimp out on the webcam as you get a 1080p front camera that supports Windows Hello face authentication.

The laptop weighs 4.19 pounds, and while its thickness can vary depending on its configuration, a maximum of 22mm isn't bad for a laptop of its caliber. The lack of gamer-esque aesthetics is a welcome addition, and its beautiful silver chassis has a decent number of ports. You get three USB Type-C connections, a USB Type-A port, a microSD card reader, an HDMI connection and even an audio jack, though users of LAN connections might want to invest in a dock as the laptop lacks a dedicated RJ45 Ethernet port.

The Gigabyte Aero 16 may not be built like your typical flashy gaming laptop, but it's easily our top pick for a high-performance OLED battlestation.

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

A beautiful screen for getting work done

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon may be the most well-known business laptop on the market, and for good reason. It has fast Intel processors and packs a solid 2.8K OLED display, all while retaining the sleek and premium business-style design ThinkPads are known for.

Pros
  • Optional 2.8K OLED display
  • 13th-generation Intel Core U- or P-series processors
  • Classic ThinkPad design is great for business
Cons
  • ThinkPad design may not be suitable for more modern audiences
  • RAM is soldered onto the laptop
$1275 at Lenovo $1720 at Best Buy $1659 at Newegg

Lenovo's ThinkPad X1 Carbon is one of the best and most iconic business laptops ever, and while the Gen 11 model doesn't change much, it's still one of the very best you can buy.

Starting with performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel Core processors, this time with the option to choose up to a Core i7-1365U or a Core i7-1370P, both supporting vPro Enterprise technologies. The U-series processors are more power-efficient than the P-series models, but they're not as fast, so you can choose based on whether you want more performance or better battery life. The laptop can also be configured with up to 64GB of RAM (which is soldered) and a 2TB SSD.

Most of the display options have remained the same, and while most configurations of the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 come with an IPS panel, you still have the option to choose a 2.8K OLED display. This is extremely sharp for a 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and of course, it has the usual benefits of OLED. You also get a 1080p webcam right above that display, with the option to add Windows Hello and Computer Vision support.

As you'd expect, the design is basically unchanged, with the black surfaces and red accents you know and (possibly) love from previous ThinkPads. It's a look that's probably divisive, but it's loved by a lot of people. What's a clear win is the portability, seeing as the ThinkPad X1 Carbon weighs just 2.48 pounds. There's a good setup of ports, too, including two Thunderbolt 4 and two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack. And if you need internet wherever you go, you can also add LTE or 5G connectivity.

It's no surprise by now, but the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is one of the best business laptops on the market, and the OLED display makes it even sweeter. We've reviewed the Gen 10 model, which is very similar aside from having older processors.

Dell XPS 15 (2023)
Best 15-inch laptop

Powerful performance in a svelte chassis

The Dell XPS 15 is one of the best 15-inch laptops, offering a great combination of performance and portability. It has powerful Intel H-series processors and up to Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 graphics packed into a relatively thin design, and of course, an optional 3.5K OLED display.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core H-series processors and Nvidia RTX graphics
  • Optional 3.5K OLED display looks stunning
  • Slim chassis
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • A little heavy
  • Design is starting to feel a little dated
$2000 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

The Dell XPS 15 has consistently been one of the best 15-inch laptops on the market, and that status remains true with the 2023 model, even though not much has changed.

Performance is the area where Dell put most of its focus this time around, upgrading to 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i9-13900H with 14 cores and 20 threads. But the real upgrade is actually the GPU, with the transition to Nvidia's RTX 40 series, up to an RTX 4070 with 8GB of memory (the 2022 model maxed out with an RTX 3050 Ti). This will make a big difference for content creation and even gaming, plus the laptop also comes with up to 64GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

Nothing else has changed with the 2023 model, but that's not necessarily bad. The 15.6-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio gives you the option for a stunning 3.5K OLED panel, which covers 100% of DCI-P3. Plus, a quad-speaker setup with up to 10W of peak output delivers fantastic audio, too. Sadly, the webcam is bad, being the same 720p sensor Dell has been using for years. It does have Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

For how powerful the Dell XPS 15 is, it's not overly large, measuring 18mm in thickness and weighing 4.23 pounds. This year, there's only one color option, with a silver exterior and black carbon fiber interior. As for ports, the setup is the same as before, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, a standard USB-C port, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader. You also get an adapter for USB Type-A and HDMI if you need those ports.

Aside from the stagnant design language, the 2023 Dell XPS 15 is still one of the very best laptops on the market, and it's more powerful than ever.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED (2023)
Best for creators

Enhance your productivity with an OLED screen

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED is a powerful laptop for creators, with 13th-generation Intel Core HX-series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics. It also has a built-in Asus dial, providing quick granular controls in certain media apps.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel Core processors and Nvidia 40-series GPUs
  • 1080p front camera with Windows Hello
  • 16:10 aspect ratio and 120Hz refresh rate
Cons
  • Default 16GB RAM can be a bottleneck
$2500 at Amazon $2500 at Best Buy $2200 at B&H

Aside from the processor, an OLED screen is perhaps the most valued feature in the photo and video editing community. There are plenty of decent OLED laptops on the market, but not a single one comes close to the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16.

Starting with the specs, the ProArt StudioBook 16 is armed with Intel's Core i9-13980HX CPU that delivers strong performance with its 5.6GHz turbo clock speed and a whooping 24 cores and 32 threads. This top-of-the-line processor is paired with an Nvidia RTX 4060 graphics card, and you can go for the high-end model with an RTX 4070 for a solid boost to your video editing and graphics rendering workloads. The default 16GB DDR5 memory can a bit on the lower side for hardware-intensive workloads, but Asus allows you to upgrade the RAM to 64GB. You also get a 1TB NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD to store all your productivity apps.

Display-wise, you get a 16-inch OLED panel with a 3200x2000 resolution and a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. Besides its 120Hz refresh rate, the touchscreen display on the ProArt StudioBook 16 boasts 100% DCI-P3 color gamut, and it supports MPP 2.0 styluses with up to 4096 pressure levels. On top of that, the laptop includes a 1080p front camera with Windows Hello for added security.

At 5.29 pounds, it's not the most portable workstation on the market, but that is to be expected considering its high-end specs. The 2023 model of the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 has limited color options, but its mineral black body offers plenty of ports, including two USB Type-A connections, two USB Type-C ports, an audio jack, a RJ45 Ethernet port, an HDMI connection, and even an SD card reader. The built-in Asus Dial increases your productivity by giving you more intuitive controls for certain apps.

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 may be a bit on the pricey side, but it's the best OLED laptop if you wish to get into content creation.

Asus Vivobook 15 OLED (2023)
Best budget laptop

A beautiful viewing experience on the cheap

$600 $700 Save $100

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED may be an affordable laptop, but it still packs AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors and an OLED display that makes it great for consuming media like movies and TV. It also looks fairly sleek, and it's decently light for its size.

Pros
  • AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors
  • OLED display in the base configuration
  • Decently light considering its size
Cons
  • No USB4 support
  • 720p webcam
  • Doesn't support DDR5 memory
$850 at Best Buy $677 at Amazon $600 at Newegg

OLED displays sued to be reserved for premium laptops, but the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED brings that great viewing experience to a much lower price point, all while being a decently modern laptop.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is powered by AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors, specifically starting with a Ryzen 7 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads. That already gives you solid day-to-day performance, and the laptop also includes 8GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD to round things out. It gives you a pretty good experience for the price.

But of course, the main draw here is that display, and even with a low price tag of $700, the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED comes with a nice 15.6-inch OLED display. It does come in a more traditional aspect ratio and Full HD resolution, but this is still a fantastic screen for watching movies and shows on, especially since it covers 100% of DCI-P3 and supports HDR. The webcam is a bit of a downside, being a 720p sensor without facial recognition, though at least the laptop has a fingerprint sensor for Windows Hello.

Design isn't really a priority with an affordable laptop, but the Vivobook 15 OLED still manages to look sleek and the 3.75 pounds of weight are not outlandish for a 15-inch laptop. It even passes MIL-STD-810H durability testing, which is really impressive. As for ports, it has one USB Type-C and three Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so there's a good bit of variety here.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is a surprisingly good laptop for the price, and getting such a good OLED display for $700 makes this a bargain. It may not have incredible performance, but for day-to-day use and watching movies, it's fantastic.

Best OLED laptops in 2023: Bottom line

It's certainly great to see OLED displays become more of a mainstay in the laptop market. While this used to be a very premium feature, these types of panels are more widely available than ever, and you can get them for very cheap. The best experience comes from the Lenovo Yoga 9i, which not only has a stunning display, but also a beautifully-crafted chassis, top-tier performance, a fantastic audio experience, and a solid webcam, all packed into a package that's still decently portable. Plus, it's a convertible, and more versatility is always a good thing.

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best overall

The best laptop on the market

$1150 $1400 Save $250

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is the best consumer laptop on the market, featuring 13th-generation Intel processors and a stunning OLED display in two different configurations. It also has a solid 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support and a beautiful shiny design.

Pros
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel Core i7 processor
  • Stunning OLED display with either 2.8K or 4K options
  • Gorgeous metal design with shiny edges
Cons
  • No place to attach a pen if needed
$1150 at Lenovo $1400 at Best Buy

With that being said, it's a fairly expensive machine, and thankfully, OLED displays come trickled down to very affordable laptops like the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED. It may not have all the bells and whistles, but it does have a beautiful OLED display with HDR support and great colors for an incredibly low price, and it doesn't make a ton of sacrifices to get there. It still has solid performance and a reasonably light design, so it's really a fantastic choice for the price.

But if you want a thin and light companion with an OLED display, then you should look no further than the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro. Despite being portable, it's a highly capable laptop with a solid Intel Core i7-1360P processor and a stunning 2.8K AMOLED display.

Otherwise, you can go up to 32GB of RAM (which is faster now) and a 2TB SSD for storage.

Unlike the laptops above, the Dell XPS 13 Plus doesn't come with an OLED display by default, but it does give you the option for a fantastic 3.5K OLED. For a 13.4-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio, that's incredibly sharp, and the screen supports touch, too. Unfortunately, the webcam, is still a 720p sensor, something Dell refuses to fix with its XPS laptops. It does support Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

After a major design shift for the 2022 model, this year's Dell XPS 13 Plus stays the same, but it still feels super modern. Features like the invisible touchpad, zero-lattice keyboard, and touch function row make this an extremely sleek laptop that looks great in both Platinum and Graphite colorways. It's a light laptop, too, weighing 2.77 pounds for the OLED model. Ports are very limited, though, with just two Thunderbolt 4 ports out of the box. You get adapters in the box for USB Type-A and a headphone jack if you need them, though this still isn't ideal.

While the futuristic design comes with some compromises, the Dell XPS 13 Plus is a phenomenal laptop in most ways, and that stunning 3.5K OLED displays pushes it even higher. We've reviewed the 2022 model, which is identical in many ways.

Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED (2023)
Great for gamers

Make your games look and run their best

$2199 $2299 Save $100

The Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED has a powerful Intel Core processor and the latest GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards paired with a 4K OLED display for an all-around great gaming experience. Unlike most gaming laptops, it's pretty thin and has a fairly minimalist design.

Pros
  • Fast Intel Core processors
  • Plenty of ports
  • Fairly thin despite its powerful specs
Cons
  • Refresh rate can be higher
  • Expensive
$2199 at Amazon $2199 at Newegg

With their vibrant colors, high contrast ratios, and wide viewing angles, OLED displays serve as great additions to gaming laptops. Unfortunately, there has been a noticeable lack of proper gaming-oriented laptops that feature OLED panels this year. Although the Gigabyte Aero 16 OLED is built like a workstation, this powerhouse of an OLED laptop is capable of running the most graphically intensive games with ease.

Performance-wise, you get what you pay for, with the base model housing a powerful Intel Core i7-13700H processor. The 14-core and 20-thread CPU boasts a maximum clock speed of 5.00GHz to deliver solid performance for all your gaming needs. But if you want even more horsepower out of your system, you can go for the Intel Core i9-13900H, which has a slightly higher clock speed of 5.40 GHz. The cheapest Aero 16 laptop comes with a modest RTX 4060 graphics card, but you can upgrade to an RTX 4070 GPU if you don't wish to turn down the graphical settings in your favorite games. The laptop also comes with 32GB DDR5 RAM and 1TB of Gen4 M.2 SSD storage to provide a well-rounded gaming experience.

The display on the Gigabyte Aero 16 is an OLED panel with a screen resolution 3840x2400, so it's pretty sharp. The tall 16:10 aspect ratio is also a plus-point, though the 60Hz refresh rate leaves a lot to be desired. Luckily, the laptop doesn't skimp out on the webcam as you get a 1080p front camera that supports Windows Hello face authentication.

The laptop weighs 4.19 pounds, and while its thickness can vary depending on its configuration, a maximum of 22mm isn't bad for a laptop of its caliber. The lack of gamer-esque aesthetics is a welcome addition, and its beautiful silver chassis has a decent number of ports. You get three USB Type-C connections, a USB Type-A port, a microSD card reader, an HDMI connection and even an audio jack, though users of LAN connections might want to invest in a dock as the laptop lacks a dedicated RJ45 Ethernet port.

The Gigabyte Aero 16 may not be built like your typical flashy gaming laptop, but it's easily our top pick for a high-performance OLED battlestation.

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

A beautiful screen for getting work done

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon may be the most well-known business laptop on the market, and for good reason. It has fast Intel processors and packs a solid 2.8K OLED display, all while retaining the sleek and premium business-style design ThinkPads are known for.

Pros
  • Optional 2.8K OLED display
  • 13th-generation Intel Core U- or P-series processors
  • Classic ThinkPad design is great for business
Cons
  • ThinkPad design may not be suitable for more modern audiences
  • RAM is soldered onto the laptop
$1275 at Lenovo $1720 at Best Buy $1659 at Newegg

Lenovo's ThinkPad X1 Carbon is one of the best and most iconic business laptops ever, and while the Gen 11 model doesn't change much, it's still one of the very best you can buy.

Starting with performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel Core processors, this time with the option to choose up to a Core i7-1365U or a Core i7-1370P, both supporting vPro Enterprise technologies. The U-series processors are more power-efficient than the P-series models, but they're not as fast, so you can choose based on whether you want more performance or better battery life. The laptop can also be configured with up to 64GB of RAM (which is soldered) and a 2TB SSD.

Most of the display options have remained the same, and while most configurations of the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 come with an IPS panel, you still have the option to choose a 2.8K OLED display. This is extremely sharp for a 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and of course, it has the usual benefits of OLED. You also get a 1080p webcam right above that display, with the option to add Windows Hello and Computer Vision support.

As you'd expect, the design is basically unchanged, with the black surfaces and red accents you know and (possibly) love from previous ThinkPads. It's a look that's probably divisive, but it's loved by a lot of people. What's a clear win is the portability, seeing as the ThinkPad X1 Carbon weighs just 2.48 pounds. There's a good setup of ports, too, including two Thunderbolt 4 and two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack. And if you need internet wherever you go, you can also add LTE or 5G connectivity.

It's no surprise by now, but the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is one of the best business laptops on the market, and the OLED display makes it even sweeter. We've reviewed the Gen 10 model, which is very similar aside from having older processors.

Dell XPS 15 (2023)
Best 15-inch laptop

Powerful performance in a svelte chassis

The Dell XPS 15 is one of the best 15-inch laptops, offering a great combination of performance and portability. It has powerful Intel H-series processors and up to Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 graphics packed into a relatively thin design, and of course, an optional 3.5K OLED display.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core H-series processors and Nvidia RTX graphics
  • Optional 3.5K OLED display looks stunning
  • Slim chassis
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • A little heavy
  • Design is starting to feel a little dated
$2000 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

The Dell XPS 15 has consistently been one of the best 15-inch laptops on the market, and that status remains true with the 2023 model, even though not much has changed.

Performance is the area where Dell put most of its focus this time around, upgrading to 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i9-13900H with 14 cores and 20 threads. But the real upgrade is actually the GPU, with the transition to Nvidia's RTX 40 series, up to an RTX 4070 with 8GB of memory (the 2022 model maxed out with an RTX 3050 Ti). This will make a big difference for content creation and even gaming, plus the laptop also comes with up to 64GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

Nothing else has changed with the 2023 model, but that's not necessarily bad. The 15.6-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio gives you the option for a stunning 3.5K OLED panel, which covers 100% of DCI-P3. Plus, a quad-speaker setup with up to 10W of peak output delivers fantastic audio, too. Sadly, the webcam is bad, being the same 720p sensor Dell has been using for years. It does have Windows Hello facial recognition, though.

For how powerful the Dell XPS 15 is, it's not overly large, measuring 18mm in thickness and weighing 4.23 pounds. This year, there's only one color option, with a silver exterior and black carbon fiber interior. As for ports, the setup is the same as before, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, a standard USB-C port, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader. You also get an adapter for USB Type-A and HDMI if you need those ports.

Aside from the stagnant design language, the 2023 Dell XPS 15 is still one of the very best laptops on the market, and it's more powerful than ever.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED (2023)
Best for creators

Enhance your productivity with an OLED screen

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 OLED is a powerful laptop for creators, with 13th-generation Intel Core HX-series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics. It also has a built-in Asus dial, providing quick granular controls in certain media apps.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel Core processors and Nvidia 40-series GPUs
  • 1080p front camera with Windows Hello
  • 16:10 aspect ratio and 120Hz refresh rate
Cons
  • Default 16GB RAM can be a bottleneck
$2500 at Amazon $2500 at Best Buy $2200 at B&H

Aside from the processor, an OLED screen is perhaps the most valued feature in the photo and video editing community. There are plenty of decent OLED laptops on the market, but not a single one comes close to the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16.

Starting with the specs, the ProArt StudioBook 16 is armed with Intel's Core i9-13980HX CPU that delivers strong performance with its 5.6GHz turbo clock speed and a whooping 24 cores and 32 threads. This top-of-the-line processor is paired with an Nvidia RTX 4060 graphics card, and you can go for the high-end model with an RTX 4070 for a solid boost to your video editing and graphics rendering workloads. The default 16GB DDR5 memory can a bit on the lower side for hardware-intensive workloads, but Asus allows you to upgrade the RAM to 64GB. You also get a 1TB NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD to store all your productivity apps.

Display-wise, you get a 16-inch OLED panel with a 3200x2000 resolution and a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. Besides its 120Hz refresh rate, the touchscreen display on the ProArt StudioBook 16 boasts 100% DCI-P3 color gamut, and it supports MPP 2.0 styluses with up to 4096 pressure levels. On top of that, the laptop includes a 1080p front camera with Windows Hello for added security.

At 5.29 pounds, it's not the most portable workstation on the market, but that is to be expected considering its high-end specs. The 2023 model of the Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 has limited color options, but its mineral black body offers plenty of ports, including two USB Type-A connections, two USB Type-C ports, an audio jack, a RJ45 Ethernet port, an HDMI connection, and even an SD card reader. The built-in Asus Dial increases your productivity by giving you more intuitive controls for certain apps.

The Asus ProArt StudioBook 16 may be a bit on the pricey side, but it's the best OLED laptop if you wish to get into content creation.

Asus Vivobook 15 OLED (2023)
Best budget laptop

A beautiful viewing experience on the cheap

$600 $700 Save $100

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED may be an affordable laptop, but it still packs AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors and an OLED display that makes it great for consuming media like movies and TV. It also looks fairly sleek, and it's decently light for its size.

Pros
  • AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors
  • OLED display in the base configuration
  • Decently light considering its size
Cons
  • No USB4 support
  • 720p webcam
  • Doesn't support DDR5 memory
$850 at Best Buy $677 at Amazon $600 at Newegg

OLED displays sued to be reserved for premium laptops, but the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED brings that great viewing experience to a much lower price point, all while being a decently modern laptop.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is powered by AMD Ryzen 7030U series processors, specifically starting with a Ryzen 7 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads. That already gives you solid day-to-day performance, and the laptop also includes 8GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD to round things out. It gives you a pretty good experience for the price.

But of course, the main draw here is that display, and even with a low price tag of $700, the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED comes with a nice 15.6-inch OLED display. It does come in a more traditional aspect ratio and Full HD resolution, but this is still a fantastic screen for watching movies and shows on, especially since it covers 100% of DCI-P3 and supports HDR. The webcam is a bit of a downside, being a 720p sensor without facial recognition, though at least the laptop has a fingerprint sensor for Windows Hello.

Design isn't really a priority with an affordable laptop, but the Vivobook 15 OLED still manages to look sleek and the 3.75 pounds of weight are not outlandish for a 15-inch laptop. It even passes MIL-STD-810H durability testing, which is really impressive. As for ports, it has one USB Type-C and three Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so there's a good bit of variety here.

The Asus Vivobook 15 OLED is a surprisingly good laptop for the price, and getting such a good OLED display for $700 makes this a bargain. It may not have incredible performance, but for day-to-day use and watching movies, it's fantastic.

Best OLED laptops in 2023: Bottom line

It's certainly great to see OLED displays become more of a mainstay in the laptop market. While this used to be a very premium feature, these types of panels are more widely available than ever, and you can get them for very cheap. The best experience comes from the Lenovo Yoga 9i, which not only has a stunning display, but also a beautifully-crafted chassis, top-tier performance, a fantastic audio experience, and a solid webcam, all packed into a package that's still decently portable. Plus, it's a convertible, and more versatility is always a good thing.

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best overall

The best laptop on the market

$1150 $1400 Save $250

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is the best consumer laptop on the market, featuring 13th-generation Intel processors and a stunning OLED display in two different configurations. It also has a solid 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support and a beautiful shiny design.

Pros
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel Core i7 processor
  • Stunning OLED display with either 2.8K or 4K options
  • Gorgeous metal design with shiny edges
Cons
  • No place to attach a pen if needed
$1150 at Lenovo $1400 at Best Buy

With that being said, it's a fairly expensive machine, and thankfully, OLED displays come trickled down to very affordable laptops like the Asus Vivobook 15 OLED. It may not have all the bells and whistles, but it does have a beautiful OLED display with HDR support and great colors for an incredibly low price, and it doesn't make a ton of sacrifices to get there. It still has solid performance and a reasonably light design, so it's really a fantastic choice for the price.

But if you want a thin and light companion with an OLED display, then you should look no further than the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro. Despite being portable, it's a highly capable laptop with a solid Intel Core i7-1360P processor and a stunning 2.8K AMOLED display.

item.title: Grab a 15.6-inch portable monitor for just $76 with this fantastic must-have deal
item.itunes:title: Grab a 15.6-inch portable monitor for just $76 with this fantastic must-have deal
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Arzopa Portable Monitor
Clip coupon for $54 discount
$76 $130 Save $54

If you're looking to expand your desktop setup or just need a light and portable monitor for when you're on the go, the Arzopa 15.6-inch monitor is going to be a great choice that provides excellent value, especially at its newly discounted price.

$76 at Amazon

A portable monitor is the perfect solution if you're looking to expand your screen real estate. Portable monitors are both thin and light, and although they used to be quite expensive, have now come down in price, making them an affordable option. Right now, you can score a 15.6-inch model for just $76 for a limited time.

Arpoza has been around for some years, and the company strictly focuses on portable monitor products, offering a range of devices in different sizes and resolutions. The 15.6-inch model is a great starting point, because it's not too big or too small, has 1080p resolution, beautiful color with support for HDR, weighs in at just 1.7 pounds, and measures 0.3 inches thick. The monitor is built using aluminum, which means it is relatively durable.

The monitor is easy to carry thanks to its included monitor case that also acts as a stand. In addition, it has a matte anti-glare IPS panel which makes it easier to see in bright environments, and has connections like USB-C and Mini HDMI, which makes it simple to connect to modern devices like smartphones and game consoles. While this monitor is slim, what might come as a surprise to some is that the monitor also has stereo speakers.

Overall, you're getting a superb package here, and with its recent discount that brings it down to just $80, it's a no-brainer. Just make sure to clip the coupon before checking out to get the discounted price.

item.title: How to use Google Switch Access to control your Android phone with switches
item.itunes:title: How to use Google Switch Access to control your Android phone with switches
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Google offers several accessibility features on Android to help users easily perform day-to-day tasks. Those with visual or auditory impairments can use the tools in the Android Accessibility Suite to navigate the UI and perform various actions without outside assistance. Among these is a handy switch-based input system called Switch Access, which lets you use your Android device with one or more switches instead of the touchscreen.

Google first launched Switch Access in 2017, and it has since picked up new functionality to help you do even more. In addition to new capabilities, Google has also split Switch Access away from the Android Accessibility Suite, and it's now available as a standalone app on the Play Store. If you face issues controlling your Android phone with its touchscreen and have yet to try out Google Switch Access, here's everything you need to get started.

How to use Google Switch Access

Switch Access is the perfect solution for those who find it difficult to directly interact with an Android device's touchscreen. You can use it to set up different hardware switches that connect to your device via USB or Bluetooth. These can then help you scan and select items on the screen, scroll through pages, type, and almost everything that would otherwise require you to interact with the touchscreen. You can even rely on the front-facing camera to recognize pre-configured facial expressions as switches.

Since the app is no longer part of the Android Accessibility Suite, you first need to install the app. You can do it via the link below. After that, follow the steps to get set up.

  1. Tap the app icon in the app drawer or select Open in the Play Store to open the app.
  2. Enable Switch Access by selecting the Settings option on the following pop-up and then tapping on the Switch Access in the Accessibility menu.
  3. Tap the toggle on the following page to enable Switch Access and then Allow on the pop-up to grant the required permissions.
    4 Images
  4. On the following page, choose the switch type you want to set up. You can select USB switch if you wish to physically connect a switch via USB, Bluetooth switch to pair a switch wirelessly over Bluetooth, or Camera Switch if you want to use facial gestures as switches.

    For this tutorial, we'll use the Camera Switch option on our Google Pixel 7 Pro.

  5. Give Switch Access permission to take pictures and record video with the front-facing camera by tapping either While using the app or Only this time on the following pop-up.
  6. You'll also have to download additional data to enable camera switches, which you can do by tapping Download on the following pop-up.
  7. After downloading the additional data, select the number of switches you wish to set up. You can use One Switch if you want to control everything with a single gesture or Two switches if you want to use one or more gestures.
    4 Images
  8. Select the scanning method on the following screen. This will allow Switch Access to highlight UI elements on your screen linearly, by row and column, or as a group.
  9. To Assign gestures for Next, choose from a variety of options, including Open Mouth, Smile, Raise Eyebrow, Look Left, Look Right, and others.
  10. To Assign gesture for Select, select the highlighted item.
  11. You can also choose a gesture on the Assign gesture for Pause, but it's optional.
    4 Images
  12. Test your switches on the following page by playing a game of Tic-Tac-Toe using just your facial gestures. Once you're satisfied with the results, tap Next and complete the setup by tapping Finish.
  13. You can make further changes to the switches by tapping the Adjust Camera Switches option on this page.
  14. After completing the setup, you can assign Switch Access shortcut. You get two options: a floating Accessibility button or pressing both volume buttons.
    4 Images

You should now be able to control your phone with facial expressions without ever needing to interact with the touch screen. If you want to make any further changes, you can jump into the Settings > Accessibility > Switch Access. The app lets you further choose menu customizations, enable or disable auto-scan, enable speech, sound, and vibration, and more.

4 Images

Control your phone with external or camera switches

Google's Switch Access app is a handy tool to help users control their Android devices without interacting with the touchscreen. It's useful for those with disabilities, but it can also come in handy if your phone's touchscreen breaks. Try it out by following the steps above.
